bids.reasoning: The negotiation has just begun, and the opponent's opening offer yields the lowest possible utility for me across all issues. To anchor the negotiation in my favor, I will counter with my optimal solution, demanding my highest-utility values on all issues to set a strong baseline before any concessions are made.

The negotiation is extremely early (t=0.02), and the opponent has repeated their initial offer, which yields my absolute lowest utility. To prompt some movement without giving up significant value, I will make a very minor concession on my least important issue (issueF) while holding firm on everything else, particularly my most critical issues (issueA, issueB, and issueC).

We are in the early stages of the negotiation (t=0.04) and the opponent is holding strictly to their opening offer, which yields minimum utility for me. To encourage them to concede and find middle ground, I will maintain a high target utility (0.961) while signaling flexibility by shifting a small concession from issueF to issueE. I will hold firm on my most highly weighted issues (A, B, and C).

The negotiation is in the very early stages (t=0.06). The opponent has rigidly repeated an offer giving me minimal utility, signaling they are testing my resolve. I will continue to make small concessions on my least important issues (D, E, F) to explore potential compromises without giving up significant overall utility. This round I will offer valueB on issueD while maintaining valueA on all others.

The negotiation is in its very early stages (t=0.08) and the opponent is repeatedly offering my worst-case scenario (all valueC) without conceding. To signal a willingness to negotiate while securing high utility for myself, I will maintain my top preferences for the most important issues (A, B, C, D) and offer a slight concession on the lowest-weight issues E and F.

The opponent is holding entirely firm on an offer that yields me the lowest possible utility across all issues, maintaining their stance through the first 10% of the negotiation. To signal a willingness to find a mutually beneficial middle ground without sacrificing my core priorities, I will propose a new permutation of minor concessions. I will maintain my top values on my highest-weight issues (A, B, C) while offering a combination of secondary values on lower-importance issues (D and E).

The opponent is repeating the identical, lowest-utility offer for me since the start of the negotiation. It is still very early (t=0.12), so I will maintain a firm stance on my highest-priority issues (issueA, issueB, issueC) while making another incremental concession on my least important issues (issueD, issueE, issueF) to explore potential compromises.

The opponent has been completely inflexible, repeatedly demanding their most preferred values across all issues. Since we are still very early in the negotiation (t=0.14), I will test their willingness to compromise by fully conceding on one of my lowest-weight issues (issueF) while holding firm on the more important ones. This maintains a very high utility for me while offering them exactly what they want on that specific issue.

The opponent is consistently repeating an offer that provides me with his maximum possible utility and minimsing mine, stubbornly holding firm without any concessions. Since the time is still very early (t=0.16) and I have a significant amount of the negotiation remaining, I will hold firm on my most valuable issues (A, B, and C). I'll continue to explore slight variations on my lowest-weighted issues (D, E, F) to signal flexibility while preserving my high overall utility. Thus, I will concede completely on issueE to 'valueC' to test their response, while keeping the rest at 'valueA'.

We are still in the early stages of the negotiation (t = 0.18), and the opponent has not budged from their initial, lowest-utility offer for me. I am continuing to methodically explore concessions on my lower-weight issues (D, E, F) to identify which issues the opponent might value highly, holding my core high-weight issues (A, B, C) at my maximum value. For this turn, I will drop the value for issue D to see if the opponent is willing to accept or reciprocate.

The opponent continues to repeat their initial, unyielding offer (all `valueC`) without any concessions. Since we are at t=0.2, I will continue to make small exploratory concessions across different issues to find where their interests lie, this time conceding on `issueB` (weight 0.2) to `valueB` while maintaining `valueA` elsewhere to preserve high overall utility.

The opponent has remained completely firm on their initial offer of 'valueC' across all issues, which provides me with my lowest possible utility (0.20). Since it is still early in the negotiation (t=0.22), there is no rush to make large concessions. I will continue probing for their preferences by offering a small concession on issueC while restoring my top value for issueB, keeping my overall utility above 0.9.

The opponent has rigidly requested `valueC` on all issues since the start, signaling a hardline or fixed strategy. With `t=0.24`, I still have plenty of time but need to test if giving them exactly what they want on a low-weight issue elicits movement. I will offer `valueC` on issueF (my lowest importance) and `valueB` on issues D and E, while maintaining `valueA` on my high-weight issues (A, B, and C) to stay above 0.84 utility.

The opponent is remaining completely rigid, repeatedly offering 'valueC' across all issues. Given the early stage of the negotiation (t=0.26), I will hold my ground on my most important issues—issueA, issueB, and issueC—which collectively represent 70% of my total utility. I will continue exploring combinations on the lower-weight issues (D, E, F) by extending minor concessions to valueB and valueC in an attempt to find some flexibility from the opponent without sacrificing much of my own utility.

The opponent continues to repeat their initial offer without making any concessions, which gives me my minimal possible utility. Because we are still early in the negotiation (t=0.28), I will maintain my highest-value options on my heavily weighted issues (A, B, and C). I will offer moderate concessions on lower-weighted issues (D and F) to signal flexibility without significantly compromising my overall utility, hoping to spur a counteroffer that moves away from their baseline.

The opponent has repeatedly proposed `valueC` across all issues since the very beginning, showing no willingness to concede so far at t=0.30. To test if they will accept a compromise on issues I care less about, I will concede to their preferred `valueC` on two of my lowest-weight issues (issueD and issueE) and offer `valueB` on issueF, while holding firm on my highest-weight issues (issueA, issueB, and issueC). This provides a substantial step toward their proposal on minor issues while protecting my core utility (targeted utility ~0.80).

The opponent has rigidly bid `valueC` for all issues without any concessions or messages since the start. At `t=0.32`, I will test their inflexibility by fully conceding on my three lowest-weight issues (D, E, F) to their requested `valueC`, while keeping my top priorities (A, B, C) at their maximum utility for me. This maintains a strong utility of 0.76 while showing a clear willingness to find a middle ground.

The opponent is holding firm on offering `valueC` for all issues in every round. Since we are at time 0.34, I need to start gradually conceding on moderate-priority issues to find a compromise, while maintaining my top-priority issue (issueA). I will give the opponent `valueC` on my lowest priority issues (D, E, F) and offer `valueB` on my moderate-priority issue B to signal flexibility.

The opponent is rigidly holding onto their opening offer of valueC for all issues, making no concessions thus far. Since we are approaching the mid-game, I will continue to signal flexibility by exchanging the previous slight concession on issueB for a slight concession on issueC (offering valueB), while maintaining full concessions on my lowest-priority issues (D, E, and F). I will hold firm on my highest-priority issue (issueA) to defend the core of my utility.

At t=0.38, the opponent remains staunchly anchored to 'valueC' across all issues, refusing to make any concessions. To break the deadlock while protecting my most critical priority, I will offer a broad compromise by dropping to 'valueB' for all issues except 'issueA'. This maintains a strong utility of ~0.73 for me and signals a willingness to meet in the middle on multiple fronts.

The time fraction is now 0.40, and the opponent has steadfastly refused to concede anything, repeating their opening offer of all `valueC`s. I will continue probing for a deal by offering the opponent's preferred `valueC` on two of my least important issues (issueE and issueF) and `valueB` on issueB and issueD, while continuing to hold firm on my most important issue (issueA).

The opponent is holding entirely firm on their opening offer demanding `valueC` perfectly on all issues, showing no flexibility thus far. Since we are approaching the midway point of the negotiation (`t=0.42`), I will try shuffling my concessions on the low-weight issues (D, E, F) and mildly on one mid-weight issue (B) while preserving my highest utility requirement on issueA. The opponent has rigidly demanded 'valueC' across all issues since the start of the negotiation, unwilling to compromise. I will retain 'valueA' for my highest-priority issue (A) and cycle issueF to 'valueA' to explore a mutually acceptable tradeoff without dropping my utility too drastically.

The opponent has repeated the exact same offer (all `valueC`) throughout the entire negotiation and has shown zero flexibility. Since we are approaching the mid-point of the negotiation, I will start making more targeted concessions to test if they will accept a compromise on my lower-weight issues (C, D, E, F). I will hold firm on my highest-priority issues (A and B) at `valueA` while offering partial concessions on their preferred values for the others to maintain an overall utility above 0.7.

As we near the midpoint of the negotiation (t=0.48), I will offer `valueC` on my three lowest-weight issues (issueD, issueE, issueF) and `valueB` on issueB, which retains a moderate utility of 0.682 for me. Holding firm on my most critical issues (issueA and issueC) allows me to gauge whether the opponent is willing to reciprocate on at least some dimensions.

I am at the midpoint of the negotiation (t=0.5). The opponent has rigidly maintained the exact same offer (all valueC) without any concessions or messages. To break the deadlock while protecting my utility, I will offer a combination where I hold firm on my most important issue (issueA) at valueA, but introduce a new mix of valueB and valueC for the remaining issues to see if the opponent has any flexibility.

The opponent has rigidly maintained the exact same offer (`valueC` for all issues) for the first half of the negotiation, showing extreme firmness. With the time fraction at 0.52, I will concede entirely to their position on my lower-weighted issues (D, E, F) by proposing `valueC`. I will also compromise partially on my medium-importance issues (B, C) with `valueB`, while solidly protecting my most important issue (A) with `valueA` to preserve a reasonable utility of ~0.606.

The opponent hasn't deviated from their initial offer, constantly demanding `valueC` on all issues since the start. At `t = 0.54`, I will continue the search for a viable middle ground by conceding fully on `issueC` (a mid-weight issue for me) to `valueC` for the first time, while keeping my most important issues (like `issueA` and `issueB`) at a higher utility. This helps probe if providing them their preferred value on `issueC` breaks the deadlock, maintaining my overall utility around 0.64.

The opponent has rigidly maintained the exact same offer of `valueC` for all issues throughout the negotiation. To break the deadlock as we pass the midpoint (t=0.56), I will try altering my stance on my most important issue (issueA) for the first time, offering `valueB`, while maintaining high utility on my secondary issues. The opponent has rigidly held to a single offer (`valueC` on all issues) for the entire negotiation so far, indicating extreme inflexibility. As we pass the midpoint (t=0.58), I need to explore different trade-offs to see if conceding fully on some of my medium/high importance issues (like issueC) can break the deadlock, while I recover utility on the low-weight issues. I will slightly adjust the values for the mid-level issues to discover if there is any latent flexibility on their end before approaching the deadline.

Assuming their preference heavily prioritizes valueC across the board, and as we reach t=0.8, I must make further concessions to avoid deadlock. The time fraction is 0.90, and the deadline is fast approaching. The time is nearing the deadline (t=0.94), reducing the opportunity for bold demands. I am offering a balanced counter that retains 'valueA' on my highest-priority issue (issueA) and 'valueB' on secondary issues, while fully giving in to their 'valueC' on all the low-priority ones, aiming for ~0.60 utility.

The deadline is extremely close at t=0.96. The opponent just offered `issueA: valueA` and `issueB: valueB`, which are my two most valuable issues, providing me with a utility of 0.522. I will accept their values for A, B, C, E, and F, but attempt one final minor value extraction by asking for `issueD: valueB`, a value they demonstrated willingness to concede at t=0.92, to slightly boost my utility to 0.563 before the deadline.

The deadline is extremely close (t=0.98). Although the opponent slightly walked back their offer on issueB in the most recent turn, they offered exactly `issueA: valueA, issueB: valueB, issueC: valueC, issueD: valueC, issueE: valueC, issueF: valueC` at t=0.96. I will propose this exact offer now to secure a utility of 0.522, which is significantly better than 0, while ensuring the opponent is highly likely to accept since they just proposed it.
